steps.title: Computational methods for predicting chemical toxicity
steps.instruction: The computational methods for estimating chemical toxicity are evolving rapidly [67]. In recent years, several models have been developed in which computational programs have been used to predict the toxicity of chemical compounds [22-24,67,68]. Quantitative structure-regulatory activity relationship (QSAR) models calculate toxicity based on the physical characteristics of the structure of chemicals such as the molecular weight or the number of benzene rings (molecular descriptors) using mathematical algorithms [69]. Following are the some examples of commercial and publicly-available models:

steps.title: Computational methods for predicting chemical toxicity
steps.instruction: •Sarah Nexus for prediction of the mutagenicity of chemicals [70].

steps.title: Computational methods for predicting chemical toxicity
steps.instruction: •VirtualToxLab for prediction of the toxic potential (endocrine and metabolic disruption, some aspects of carcinogenicity and cardiotoxicity) of drugs, chemicals and natural products [71].

steps.title: Computational methods for predicting chemical toxicity
steps.instruction: •Toxicity Estimation Software Tool (TEST) for prediction of the acute toxicity of organic chemicals based on their molecular structures [72].

steps.title: Computational methods for predicting chemical toxicity
steps.instruction: •TOPKAT for prediction of the ecotoxicity, mutagenicity, and reproductive/developmental toxicity of chemicals [73].

steps.title: Computational methods for predicting chemical toxicity
steps.instruction: •Ecological Structure Activity Relationships (ECOSAR) for estimation of the aquatic toxicity (acute short-term), toxicity and chronic (long-term or delayed) toxicity of industrial chemicals to aquatic organisms such as fish, aquatic invertebrates, green algae and aquatic plants by using computerized structure activity relationships [74]

steps.title: Computational methods for predicting chemical toxicity
steps.instruction: •Estimation Programs Interface (EPI) suite for prediction of physical/chemical properties and environmental fate (eco-toxicity). The software calculates chemical property data using programs including KOWWIN, AOPWIN, HENRYWIN, MPBPWIN, BIOWIN, KOCWIN, WSKOWWIN, WATERNT, BCFBAF, HYDROWIN and ECOSAR [75].

steps.title: Computational methods for predicting chemical toxicity
steps.instruction: •CAESAR for assessment of chemical toxicity under the REACH [76].

steps.title: Computational methods for predicting chemical toxicity
steps.instruction: •ToxiPred: A server for prediction of aqueous toxicity of small chemical molecules in Tetrahymena pyriformis[77].
